skill.name: document-writing-coordination
skill.description: Coordinates large documentation projects by outlining sections, creating VibeKanban tasks, delegating writing work, and reviewing section quality.
skill.summary: Coordinate multi-section technical documents with delegated writers and structured review loops.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown fences, inline paths, placeholder security terms, and task templates. The skill does not contain executable scripts, hidden network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text. Residual risk is low because it coordinates external agents and VibeKanban tasks, so users should confirm tool access before delegation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown code fences, inline file paths, and MCP task examples. They document how to create and update VibeKanban tasks and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are Markdown examples and fenced text blocks, not executable Ruby or shell code. No script file or command interpreter invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Security and Crypto Terms Used as Documentation Topics
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and C2-style indicators, but the cited content discusses documentation sections, source file references, and example benchmark topics. These terms describe what writers should document, not unsafe cryptographic implementation or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are documentation templates and examples. I found no cryptographic algorithm implementation, C2 endpoint, persistence mechanism, or encoded payload.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Terms Are Part of Documentation Review Workflow
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance patterns for lines that ask writers to inspect project structure, validate links, or verify file references. This is expected for a documentation coordination skill and does not instruct stealthy discovery or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is document production and review. The skill asks agents to read project files for accuracy, with no collection of secrets or transmission outside the workspace.

content.value_statement: Large technical documents are hard to split, track, and review across many contributors. This skill creates section plans, delegates writing through VibeKanban, and keeps reviews consistent.
actual_capabilities: Creates a structured document outline with section scopes and placeholders.
Builds VibeKanban task descriptions for individual document sections.
Defines when large sections should be split into smaller writing tasks.
Tracks task states from todo through inreview and done.
Reviews completed sections for accuracy, completeness, style, and consistency.
Provides reusable templates for standard, technical, overview, utility, and security sections.
limitations: Requires VibeKanban MCP tools for the full task orchestration workflow.
Does not write final document content directly when following its coordinator role.
Depends on separate writer agents to use the requested coauthoring skill correctly.
Cannot verify technical accuracy without access to the relevant project files.

use_cases.title: Architecture Document Program
use_cases.description: Split a large architecture document into scoped sections, assign writers, and review each merge.

use_cases.title: Release Documentation Push
use_cases.description: Coordinate several contributors who must update feature, testing, and migration sections before release.

use_cases.title: Repository Documentation Audit
use_cases.description: Create tasks that ask writers to inspect source files and fill gaps in project documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Document Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an outline for a technical design document for this repository. Include section scopes, placeholders, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Delegate Existing Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this document outline and create VibeKanban tasks for each pending section. Keep each task self-contained for a fresh writer agent.

prompt_templates.title: Review Completed Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Review Section 3.1 against its task scope and source files. Decide whether to approve it or send specific revision feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Run Full Coordination Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Coordinate the full writing process for this architecture document. Create tasks, start writer attempts, monitor status, review completed sections, and run a consistency pass.

output_examples.input: Create a design document plan for a repository with several services.
output_examples.output: A table of contents with scoped sections, TODO placeholders, source areas to inspect, and delegation-ready section boundaries.

output_examples.input: Delegate Section 4 about the authentication service.
output_examples.output: A VibeKanban task description with context, source files, required output, acceptance criteria, and a review status target.

output_examples.input: Review the completed testing strategy section.
output_examples.output: An approval or revision decision with accuracy, completeness, formatting, and consistency notes.

best_practices: Give each section task enough context for a writer with no prior conversation history.
Set clear scope boundaries and acceptance criteria before starting writer agents.
Run a consistency review after each approved section changes the main document.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad tasks that ask one writer to handle the whole document.
Do not approve sections without checking them against source files and the assigned scope.
Do not let terminology drift across sections after multiple writers contribute.

faq.question: What does this skill coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates multi-section technical documents by planning, delegating, monitoring, reviewing, and checking consistency.

faq.question: Does it write the document itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill defines a coordinator role that delegates writing to separate doc writer agents.

faq.question: Does it require VibeKanban?
faq.answer: VibeKanban is required for the full workflow. A fallback Task tool flow is described when repositories are not configured.

faq.question: What documents fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits architecture documents, design documents, release documentation, and repository guides with many sections.

faq.question: How does it maintain quality?
faq.answer: It uses review checklists for accuracy, completeness, formatting, cross-references, and terminology consistency.

faq.question: Can it handle very large sections?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes delegation rules that split oversized sections into smaller subtasks.
